# Critical Pre-Implementation Checklist: CRUD de Planos de Assinatura Master

**Purpose**: Validate whether the SubscriptionPlan CRUD requirements, plan, and tasks are ready for implementation without hidden scope, layering, money, authorization, or test gaps.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all requested CRUD user stories documented with independent tests and acceptance scenarios? [Completeness, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK002 Are all five requested endpoints explicitly specified with request and response expectations? [Completeness, Spec §API Endpoints and Payloads]
- [x] CHK003 Are expected validation, not-found, conflict, and internal-error responses documented for the relevant operations? [Completeness, Spec §Expected Errors]
- [x] CHK004 Is the backoffice master access requirement internally consistent with the explicit restriction not to alter authentication or create new authorization middleware? [Conflict, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Expected Errors, Plan §Authorization]

## Domain And Money Requirements

- [x] CHK005 Is `SubscriptionPlan` clearly required to remain an independent Entity rather than an Aggregate Root? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK006 Is reuse of the existing `MoneyInCents` value object explicitly required instead of defining another monetary type? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK007 Are monetary requirements clear that `priceInCents` is the contract and that float, decimal, or formatted money values are not accepted? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, Spec §VR-009]
- [x] CHK008 Are operator and patient limits defined as positive integers in both functional requirements and validation rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010, Spec §VR-005, Spec §VR-006]
- [x] CHK009 Is optional description behavior documented clearly enough to distinguish omitted/null descriptions from blank sent descriptions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-012, Spec §VR-003]

## Layering And Dependency Boundaries

- [x] CHK010 Are Domain dependency boundaries explicitly documented so Domain remains independent of Fastify, Prisma, Zod, and HTTP? [Consistency, Spec §FR-019, Plan §Target Architecture]
- [x] CHK011 Are Application dependency boundaries documented so use cases depend on repository interfaces rather than Prisma directly? [Consistency, Spec §FR-020, Plan §Target Architecture]
- [x] CHK012 Are Infrastructure responsibilities documented as the home for Prisma repositories and mappers? [Consistency, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Target Architecture]
- [x] CHK013 Are Presentation responsibilities documented as the home for Fastify routes/handlers and Zod request schemas? [Consistency, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Target Architecture]
- [x] CHK014 Are package boundaries clear that only `packages/api` is affected and frontend/shared package changes are out unless forced by existing architecture? [Completeness, Spec §Constitution Alignment, Plan §Monorepo Boundaries]

## Scope Control

- [x] CHK015 Are Organization changes explicitly bounded to the existing plan reference and delete-in-use guard? [Scope, Spec §Out of Scope, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK016 Are authentication, authorization middleware, billing, frontend, shared-contract promotion, pagination, filtering, and Aggregate Root promotion explicitly excluded? [Scope, Spec §Out of Scope, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK017 Is the Prisma nullability mismatch for `description` identified with a plan/task path before implementation? [Dependency, Plan §Current Architecture Analysis, Tasks §T002/T006]

## Task Quality

- [x] CHK018 Are tasks separated by Domain, Application, Infrastructure, Presentation, and tests while still grouped by user story for independent delivery? [Completeness, Tasks §Phases 1-7]
- [x] CHK019 Are tasks small, objective, file-scoped, and written with objective, dependencies, acceptance criteria, and tests? [Clarity, Tasks §Format]
- [x] CHK020 Are overlapping edits to shared files such as repository, mapper, factory, schemas, and route file ordered safely rather than parallelized unsafely? [Consistency, Tasks §Dependencies & Execution Order]
- [x] CHK021 Are implementation tasks ordered safely from domain and schema alignment through repository, use cases, infrastructure, presentation, registration, and validation gates? [Coverage, Tasks §Implementation Order Alignment]

## Verification And Rollback

- [x] CHK022 Are minimum automated tests specified for value objects/entities, use cases, Zod schemas, and optional HTTP integration when a project pattern exists? [Completeness, Tasks §Tests, Plan §Testing]
- [x] CHK023 Are typecheck, lint, Prisma generation, API tests, build, and optional repo-wide gates documented as validation commands? [Completeness, Plan §Validation Commands, Tasks §T042-T046]
- [x] CHK024 Is rollback strategy documented for route registration, new files, repository/mapper extensions, schema migration, and error-handler changes? [Completeness, Plan §Rollback Strategy]

## Execution Finding

Classification: **Pode implementar agora**

Reason: CHK004 resolved. The spec and OpenAPI contract now define `/backoffice` as a technical namespace/prefix only for this slice. The feature no longer requires `403 Backoffice Master Required`, authorization enforcement, permission middleware, or Master profile validation.

Re-run: 2026-06-18

No remaining required adjustment before implementation.
